3A_Bibliographic.citation: Bagot, K. S., Matthews, S. A., Mason, M., Squeglia, L. M., Fowler, J., Gray, K., Herting, M., May, A., Colrain, I., Godino, J., Tapert, S., Brown, S., & Patrick, K. (2018). Current, future and potential use of mobile and wearable technologies and social media data in the ABCD study to increase understanding of contributors to child health. Developmental Cognitive Neuroscience, 32, 121-129.
3A_Bibliographic.notes: Author list inferred from DOI metadata. This is a methods/perspective paper, NOT an empirical study.

3B_Sample_Data.abcd_release: N/A - perspective/methods paper, no data analyzed
3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: N/A - This is a methodological perspective paper describing current, future, and potential use of mobile and wearable technologies in the ABCD study. No empirical data are analyzed.
3B_Sample_Data.notes: No original data analysis. This paper describes methods and plans for incorporating mobile technologies into ABCD.

3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: N/A - This paper discusses potential use of EMA, SMS texting, continuous passive data collection, wearable sensors, GPS, social media data, and environmental sensors in the ABCD study. No empirical screen time exposure is analyzed.

3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: N/A - No outcomes analyzed. Paper discusses potential for studying substance use, mental health, physical activity, sleep, and environmental exposures using mobile technologies.

3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: N/A - This paper reviews methodological approaches (EMA, wearable sensors, GPS, social media analysis, biosensors) and discusses their potential implementation in the ABCD study. No empirical analyses are presented.
3E_Design_Analysis.notes: This paper may not meet inclusion criteria for the systematic review as it contains no empirical data on screen time associations.

3G_Reporting_Quality.notes: No empirical data or statistical results to evaluate.

3H_Recommendations.notes: Paper makes methodological recommendations for future ABCD research but no clinical/public health recommendations about screen time.

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: This is a perspective/methods paper that describes how mobile and wearable technologies could be used in the ABCD study to measure substance use, physical activity, environmental exposures, social media behavior, and other health-relevant data. It discusses privacy/ethical issues and practical implementation challenges. No empirical findings about screen time or social media effects are presented or discussed.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.ambiguity_notes: This paper is a methods/perspective piece with no empirical data. It likely should be excluded from the systematic review as it does not contain any original data on screen time associations with outcomes.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.notes: FLAGGED FOR EXCLUSION: This is a perspective/methods paper describing potential future use of mobile technologies in ABCD. It contains no empirical data on screen time or social media associations with any outcomes.
